items.title: CVE-2025-0282: Ivanti Connect Secure Stack Buffer Overflow
items.description: A stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure before version 22.7R2.5 allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to achieve remote code execution. CISA has added this to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

items.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
items.description: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. These vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise.

items.title: New EAGERBEE Malware Variant Targeting Middle East ISPs and Government Entities
items.description: Security researchers have discovered a new variant of the EAGERBEE backdoor framework targeting internet service providers and government entities in the Middle East. The malware includes novel plugin components for file system manipulation and remote access.

items.title: CVE-2024-49113: Windows LDAP Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
items.description: A critical remote code execution vulnerability exists in Windows Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP). An unauthenticated attacker could exploit this by sending specially crafted LDAP requests to the target server.

items.title: FunkSec Ransomware Group Claims 85 Victims in December Using AI-Assisted Malware
items.description: A newly emerged ransomware group called FunkSec has claimed responsibility for attacks on 85 organizations in December 2024. Researchers believe the group is using AI-generated code to develop their malware, lowering the technical barrier to ransomware development.

items.title: Microsoft January 2025 Patch Tuesday: 159 Vulnerabilities Fixed
items.description: Microsoft has released its January 2025 Patch Tuesday security updates addressing 159 vulnerabilities, including 10 critical remote code execution flaws and 3 zero-days actively exploited in the wild. Administrators are urged to prioritize patching immediately.

items.title: SANS ISC: Handler's Diary — Scanning for Exposed Git Repositories
items.description: Threat actors are actively scanning for publicly exposed .git directories that could leak source code, credentials, and configuration files. SANS researchers observed a significant spike in scanning activity targeting web servers for misconfigured git repositories.

items.title: Thousands of Fortinet Devices Backdoored via Symlink Exploit
items.description: Security researchers have discovered that threat actors have planted a persistent backdoor mechanism on thousands of Fortinet devices by creating a symbolic link that bypasses firmware updates. Affected organizations may remain compromised even after patching CVE-2023-27997.

items.title: CVE-2025-0283: Ivanti Connect Secure Privilege Escalation
items.description: A privilege escalation vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure before version 22.7R2.5 allows a local authenticated attacker to elevate privileges to root. This vulnerability is related to CVE-2025-0282 and is being exploited together in the same attack chains.

items.title: North Korean Hackers Steal $308 Million in Bitcoin from DMM Exchange
items.description: US and Japanese law enforcement agencies have jointly attributed the theft of $308 million in Bitcoin from Japanese cryptocurrency exchange DMM Bitcoin to North Korean state-sponsored hackers (TraderTraitor / Lazarus Group). The attack involved social engineering and supply chain compromise.

items.title: r/netsec: Proof-of-concept for Apache Struts RCE (CVE-2024-53677) released
items.description: A working proof-of-concept exploit for the recently disclosed Apache Struts remote code execution vulnerability has been released publicly. Security teams should treat this as actively exploited and patch or apply mitigations immediately.

items.title: CISA Emergency Directive: Agencies Must Mitigate Ivanti Vulnerabilities
items.description: CISA has issued an emergency directive requiring all federal civilian executive branch agencies to immediately disconnect Ivanti Connect Secure and Ivanti Policy Secure appliances from their networks, citing active exploitation of multiple critical vulnerabilities.

items.title: New Banshee Stealer macOS Malware Uses Apple XProtect Encryption to Evade Detection
items.description: A new variant of Banshee macOS Stealer has been observed using the same string encryption algorithm as Apple's XProtect antivirus engine to evade detection. The malware targets browser credentials, cryptocurrency wallets, and iCloud Keychain data.

items.title: CVE-2024-55591: FortiOS Authentication Bypass — Actively Exploited
items.description: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Fortinet FortiOS and FortiProxy allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to gain super-admin privileges via crafted requests to the Node.js websocket module. Mass exploitation observed in the wild targeting management interfaces.

items.title: PowerSchool Data Breach Exposes Student and Teacher Records Across North America
items.description: Education technology company PowerSchool has disclosed a data breach affecting school districts across the United States and Canada. The breach exposed personal information of students and teachers, potentially affecting millions of individuals. Attackers used compromised credentials to access the PowerSchool Student Information System.

items.title: SANS ISC: Analyzing Encrypted Malware C2 Traffic with Zeek and Suricata
items.description: A practical guide to detecting encrypted command-and-control communications from malware infections using open-source network monitoring tools. SANS researchers demonstrate detection techniques for common malware families that use TLS-encrypted C2 channels.

items.title: CVE-2024-49138: Windows Common Log File System Privilege Escalation (0-day)
items.description: A privilege escalation vulnerability in the Windows Common Log File System (CLFS) driver was exploited as a zero-day before the December 2024 Patch Tuesday. Attackers used this vulnerability to gain SYSTEM-level privileges on compromised machines.

items.title: MirrorFace APT Expands Operations to Target European Diplomatic Entities
items.description: The Chinese nation-state threat actor MirrorFace (also tracked as Earth Kasha) has expanded operations beyond Japan to target diplomatic organizations in Europe. The group uses spear-phishing emails delivering ANEL and NOOPDOOR backdoors for persistent access.

items.title: US Treasury Sanctions Chinese Firm and Hacker for Salt Typhoon Telecom Breaches
items.description: The US Treasury Department has imposed sanctions on a Chinese cybersecurity company and one of its employees for their alleged roles in the Salt Typhoon intrusions that compromised major US telecommunications providers, enabling surveillance of American citizens and government officials.

items.title: CISA Alert: Oracle WebLogic Server Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: CISA has issued an alert warning that threat actors are actively exploiting a deserialization vulnerability in Oracle WebLogic Server. Federal agencies and critical infrastructure operators are urged to apply available patches or implement recommended mitigations immediately.
